description: §6.7.7 <request> settled-state machine, SUCCESS path (Peter #20 / GitHub #20). A body-form `<request id="userReq">${ @userData = loadUser() }</>` calls a server fn returning a User. On a 2xx settle: `<#userReq>.loading` transitions to false (#loading reads "false" — was STUCK at initial `true` pre-fix), `<#userReq>.data` populates so `if=<#userReq>.data` shows #who, the assigned @userData cell hydrates from .data (#who reads the name), and `<#userReq>.error` stays absent so `if=<#userReq>.error` keeps #err out of the DOM. Twin of request-settle-error-rt.
rationale: §6.7.7 settled(success): .loading→false, .data→resolved value, .error remains not, .stale→false; the assigned @cell is the sanctioned compiler-managed write point populated from .data. Pre-fix the compiler emitted the request-state object + read sites but NO transition code, so none of these settled — the state machine was inert (Peter #20).
